title: Fourier Neural Operator for Parametric Partial Differential Equations
abstract: We propose a new framework for learning the solution operator of parametric PDEs using Fourier neural operators (FNO). The FNO learns a mapping between function spaces by parameterizing the integral kernel in Fourier space. It is the first ML-based method to successfully model turbulent flows with zero-shot super-resolution.
method_summary: Introduces the Fourier Neural Operator that learns mappings between infinite-dimensional function spaces via spectral convolutions, achieving up to three orders of magnitude speedup over traditional PDE solvers.

title: Learning nonlinear operators via DeepONet based on the universal approximation theorem of operators
abstract: We propose DeepONet, a deep learning framework for learning diverse continuous nonlinear operators from data. DeepONet is based on the universal approximation theorem of operators, which states that a neural network with a single hidden layer can approximate any nonlinear continuous operator.
method_summary: Introduces DeepONet architecture with branch and trunk networks for learning nonlinear operators, demonstrating effectiveness on integrals, fractional Laplacians, and differential equations.

title: Neural operators for accelerating scientific simulations and design
abstract: Neural operators can extrapolate and predict solutions at new locations unseen during training. They can be integrated with physics and other domain constraints enforced at finer resolutions to obtain accurate solutions.
method_summary: Comprehensive review of neural operator architectures for scientific computing, covering FNO, DeepONet, and their applications in accelerating simulations and engineering design.

title: Learning nonlinear operators in latent spaces for real-time predictions of complex dynamics in physical systems
abstract: We propose an approach for learning neural operators in latent spaces, facilitating real-time predictions for highly nonlinear and multiscale systems on high-dimensional domains. Our method utilizes variational autoencoders to map high-dimensional data to a compact latent representation.
method_summary: Proposes latent space neural operators combining VAE-based dimensionality reduction with operator learning for real-time prediction of complex multiscale physical systems.

title: A Practical Introduction to Neural Operators in Scientific Computing
abstract: This focused review explores a range of neural operator architectures for approximating solutions to parametric partial differential equations, emphasizing high-level concepts and practical implementation. Beyond forward problem-solving, the review delves into applying neural operators as surrogates in Bayesian inference problems.
method_summary: Practical tutorial-style review of neural operator architectures including FNO, DeepONet, and their variants, with emphasis on implementation guidance for scientific computing applications.

title: Equilibrium Neural Operator: A Physics-Informed Neural Operator for Multiscale Simulations
abstract: We propose Equilibrium Neural Operator (EquiNO) as a complementary physics-informed PDE surrogate for predicting microscale physics and compare it with variational physics-informed approaches.
method_summary: Introduces a physics-informed neural operator specifically designed for multiscale simulations, embedding equilibrium constraints directly into the operator learning framework.

title: NOEM: Efficient and Scalable Finite Element Method Enabled by Reusable Neural Operators
abstract: NOEM leverages neural operators to simulate subdomains where a large number of finite elements would be required if FEM was used. In each subdomain, a neural operator is used to build a single element, namely a neural-operator element (NOE). NOEs are then integrated with standard finite elements to represent the entire solution domain.
method_summary: Proposes a hybrid approach replacing dense FEM subdomains with reusable neural-operator elements, dramatically reducing computational cost while maintaining accuracy through integration with standard FEM.

title: Finite Operator Learning: Bridging Neural Operators and Numerical Methods for Efficient Parametric Solution and Optimization of PDEs
abstract: We introduce a method that combines neural operators, physics-informed machine learning, and standard numerical methods for solving PDEs. The proposed approach unifies aforementioned methods and can be applied to parametric solution and optimization problems.
method_summary: Unifies neural operators with classical numerical methods and physics-informed learning into a single framework for efficient parametric PDE solution and optimization.

title: An FEA Surrogate Model with Boundary Oriented Graph Embedding Approach for Rapid Design
venue: Journal of Computational Design and Engineering
abstract: We present a Boundary Oriented Graph Embedding (BOGE) approach for the Graph Neural Network (GNN) to assist in rapid design and digital prototyping. The cantilever beam problem has been used to demonstrate the approach.
method_summary: Introduces BOGE, a graph embedding method that encodes boundary conditions into GNN-based FEA surrogates, enabling rapid structural design evaluation without full FEA computation.

title: A Comprehensive Evaluation of Graph Neural Networks and Physics Informed Learning for Surrogate Modelling of Finite Element Analysis
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive evaluation of graph neural networks (GNNs) and 3D U-Nets as surrogates for FEA of parametric I-beams. We introduce a Physics-Informed Neural Network (PINN) framework for structural analysis.
method_summary: Benchmarks GNN and 3D U-Net architectures as FEA surrogates on parametric structural problems, providing systematic comparison of accuracy, generalization, and computational efficiency.

title: A Comprehensive Comparison of Neural Operators for 3D Industry-Scale Engineering Designs
abstract: Neural operators have emerged as powerful tools for learning nonlinear mappings between function spaces, enabling real-time prediction of complex dynamics in diverse scientific and engineering applications.
method_summary: Provides the first large-scale benchmark comparing neural operator architectures (FNO, DeepONet, etc.) on realistic 3D industrial engineering design problems.

title: A Graph Neural Network Surrogate for 3D Finite Element Modeling: Accelerated Full-Field Parameter Identification in Aluminum Alloy 6DR1
venue: Modelling and Simulation in Materials Science and Engineering
abstract: This study introduces a novel three-dimensional graph neural network (GNN) based finite element (FE) surrogate model designed to emulate full-field tensile simulations of aluminum alloy 6DR1.
method_summary: Develops a 3D GNN surrogate that replaces full FE simulations for material parameter identification, achieving significant speedup in full-field stress/strain prediction.

title: A Physics-Encoded Fourier Neural Operator Approach for Surrogate Modeling of Divergence-Free Stress Fields in Solids
abstract: The purpose of the current work is the development of a so-called physics-encoded Fourier neural operator (PeFNO) for surrogate modeling of the quasi-static equilibrium stress field in solids.
method_summary: Introduces PeFNO that encodes divergence-free constraints directly into the Fourier neural operator architecture for physically consistent stress field prediction in polycrystalline materials.

title: Learning Deep Implicit Fourier Neural Operators (IFNOs) with Applications to Heterogeneous Material Modeling
abstract: To model the complex responses due to material heterogeneity and defects, we develop a novel deep neural operator architecture, which we coin as the Implicit Fourier Neural Operator (IFNO).
method_summary: Extends FNO with implicit layers for modeling complex material responses in heterogeneous media, improving accuracy on problems with sharp interfaces and defects.

title: Neural Operators for Stochastic Modeling of Nonlinear Structural System Response to Natural Hazards
abstract: In this work, we employ two state-of-the-art neural operators, the deep operator network (DeepONet) and the Fourier neural operator (FNO) for the prediction of the nonlinear time history response of structures under natural hazards.
method_summary: Applies DeepONet and FNO to predict stochastic nonlinear structural responses under earthquake and wind loads, demonstrating neural operators for reliability analysis.

title: Toward Reusable Surrogate Models: Graph-Based Transfer Learning on Trusses
abstract: Surrogate models have several uses in engineering design, including speeding up design optimization, noise reduction, test measurement interpolation, gradient estimation, portability, and protection of proprietary data.
method_summary: Proposes graph-based transfer learning for reusable surrogate models across different truss topologies, enabling knowledge transfer between structural design problems.

title: Shape-Informed Surrogate Models Based on Signed Distance Function Domain Encoding
abstract: We propose a non-intrusive method to build surrogate models that approximate the solution of parameterized partial differential equations (PDEs), capable of taking into account the dependence of the solution on the shape of the domain.
method_summary: Encodes domain geometry via signed distance functions as input to neural surrogates, enabling generalization across different geometric configurations without retraining.

title: Learning Mesh-Based Simulation with Graph Networks
abstract: We introduce MeshGraphNets, a framework for learning mesh-based simulations using graph neural networks. Our model can be trained to pass messages on a mesh graph and to adapt the mesh discretization during forward simulation.
method_summary: Introduces MeshGraphNets that learn to simulate physics on unstructured meshes via message-passing GNNs, with adaptive mesh refinement capability, achieving high fidelity across fluid and structural domains.

title: Data-Driven Turbulence Modeling: A Survey
abstract: We briefly recall the origin and limitations of RANS models, and then review their shortcomings and uncertainties. Then, we provide an introduction to data-driven approaches to RANS turbulence closure modeling.
method_summary: Comprehensive survey of data-driven approaches to turbulence modeling, covering neural network-based RANS closures, symbolic regression methods, and hybrid physics-ML strategies.

title: Turbulence Closure Modeling with Machine Learning Approaches: A Review
abstract: To do full justice to the potential of data-driven approaches in turbulence modeling, this article seeks to identify the foundational physics challenges underlying turbulence closure modeling and assess how machine learning can address them.
method_summary: Reviews the intersection of machine learning and turbulence closure modeling, identifying key physics challenges and evaluating how deep learning, symbolic regression, and hybrid methods address RANS limitations.

title: A Forward Look on AI Foundation Models in Computational Fluid Dynamics
abstract: Driven by the advancement of GPUs and AI, the field of Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) is undergoing significant transformations. This paper bridges the gap between the machine learning and CFD communities.
method_summary: Proposes a vision for AI foundation models in CFD, discussing how large-scale pretrained models can transform fluid simulation through transfer learning and multi-task capabilities.

title: Accelerating Transient CFD through Machine Learning-Based Flow Initialization
abstract: We present a novel machine learning-based initialization method that reduces the cost of subsequent transient CFD solves substantially, achieving a 50% reduction in time-to-convergence compared to traditional initialization.
method_summary: Uses ML models to generate intelligent initial conditions for transient CFD simulations, cutting convergence time in half by starting closer to the physical solution.

title: Physics-Constrained DeepONet for Surrogate CFD Models
abstract: This methodology is exemplified through surrogate modeling of fluid dynamics over a curved backward-facing step, a benchmark problem in computational fluid dynamics. The model was trained on computational data.
method_summary: Extends DeepONet with physics constraints (conservation laws) for CFD surrogate modeling, demonstrating improved accuracy on benchmark flow problems compared to unconstrained variants.

title: Neural Fields for Rapid Aircraft Aerodynamics Simulations
abstract: This paper presents a methodology to learn surrogate models of steady state fluid dynamics simulations on meshed domains, based on Implicit Neural Representations (INRs).
method_summary: Applies implicit neural representations as continuous surrogate models for aircraft aerodynamics, enabling rapid evaluation of flow fields at arbitrary spatial resolutions.

title: Physics-Based Simulations with Masked Graph Neural Networks
abstract: We introduce a novel masked pre-training technique for graph neural networks (GNNs) applied to computational fluid dynamics (CFD) problems. By randomly masking up to 40% of input mesh nodes during pre-training, the model learns robust representations.
method_summary: Proposes masked pre-training for GNNs in CFD, improving generalization and data efficiency by learning to reconstruct masked mesh regions during self-supervised pre-training.

title: The Neural Particle Method -- An Updated Lagrangian Physics Informed Neural Network for Computational Fluid Dynamics
abstract: It has recently been shown that instead of solving a system of equations as in standard numerical methods, a neural network can be trained solely based on initial and boundary conditions. Neural networks offer a mesh-free alternative for solving PDEs.
method_summary: Introduces a mesh-free Lagrangian PINN approach for CFD that tracks fluid particles, combining physics-informed training with particle-based discretization for free-surface flows.

title: Scalable Multi-Scale Graph Neural Networks for Physics Simulation
abstract: X-MeshGraphNet overcomes the scalability bottleneck by partitioning large graphs and incorporating halo regions that enable seamless message passing across partitions. This, combined with gradient aggregation, enables training on meshes with millions of nodes.
method_summary: Extends MeshGraphNets with multi-scale graph partitioning and halo-based message passing, enabling physics simulation on industrial-scale meshes with millions of nodes.

title: Physics-Informed Neural Networks: A Deep Learning Framework for Solving Forward and Inverse Problems Involving Nonlinear PDEs
abstract: We introduce physics-informed neural networks -- neural networks that are trained to solve supervised learning tasks while respecting any given laws of physics described by general nonlinear partial differential equations. We present two classes of algorithms: continuous time and discrete time models.
method_summary: Foundational PINN paper that embeds PDE residuals as soft constraints in neural network training, enabling mesh-free solution of forward and inverse problems without labeled simulation data.

title: Physics-Informed Neural Operator for Learning Partial Differential Equations
abstract: We propose physics-informed neural operators (PINO) that combine training data and physics constraints to learn the solution operator of a given family of parametric Partial Differential Equations. PINO uses the Fourier neural operator (FNO) framework.
method_summary: Combines physics-informed training with neural operator architecture (FNO), enabling operator learning with fewer data samples by incorporating PDE residual losses.

title: Physics-Informed Neural Networks for Solving Thermo-Mechanics Problems of Functionally Graded Material
abstract: This study uses PINNs to solve coupled thermo-mechanics problems of materials with functionally graded properties. An in-depth analysis of the PINN framework has been carried out by understanding the effect of network architecture and training strategies.
method_summary: Applies PINNs to coupled thermal-mechanical analysis of functionally graded materials, demonstrating mesh-free solution of multi-physics problems with spatially varying material properties.

title: Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs) for Heat Transfer Problems
abstract: In PINNs, automatic differentiation is leveraged to evaluate differential operators without discretization errors, and a multitask learning problem is defined in order to simultaneously fit observed data while respecting physics constraints.
method_summary: Demonstrates PINN application to heat transfer problems including conduction, convection, and radiation, showing how physics constraints improve prediction accuracy with sparse data.

title: Physics-Informed Neural Networks and Extensions: A Review
abstract: In this paper, we review the new method Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs) that has become the main pillar in scientific machine learning, we present recent practical extensions, and provide a structured overview of the field.
method_summary: Comprehensive review of PINN methodology and its extensions including domain decomposition, multi-fidelity, and adaptive training strategies for engineering applications.

title: Scientific Machine Learning Through Physics-Informed Neural Networks: Where We Are and What's Next
abstract: Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINN) are neural networks that encode model equations, like Partial Differential Equations (PDE), as a component of the neural network itself. PINNs are nowadays used to solve PDEs, fractional equations, integral-differential equations.
method_summary: Survey of PINN methodology covering theoretical foundations, training strategies, and applications across structural, thermal, and fluid mechanics domains.

title: Physics-Informed Neural Networks: From Fundamentals to Applications in Complex Systems
abstract: Physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) have emerged as a versatile and widely applicable concept within the realm of artificial intelligence, impacting various science and engineering domains over the past few years.
method_summary: Reviews PINN applications in complex engineering systems including structural dynamics, thermal management, and multi-physics coupling, with focus on scalability challenges.

title: Equation Discovery, Parametric Simulation, and Optimization Using PINN for Heat Conduction
abstract: In the modeling phase, the governing equation of heat transfer by conduction is reconstructed through equation discovery using fractional-order derivatives, enabling the identification of the fractional order from data.
method_summary: Uses PINNs for simultaneous equation discovery and optimization in heat conduction problems, combining inverse problem solving with design optimization in a unified framework.

title: TopoDiff: Diffusion Models Beat GANs on Topology Optimization
abstract: We propose TopoDiff - a conditional diffusion-model-based architecture to perform performance-aware and manufacturability-aware topology optimization. Our model introduces a new guidance strategy to make diffusion models performance-aware and constraints-aware.
method_summary: First application of conditional diffusion models to topology optimization, outperforming GANs with a novel physics-guided sampling strategy that ensures structural performance and manufacturability.

title: Transformer-Based Topology Optimization
abstract: Topology optimization enables the design of highly efficient and complex structures, but conventional iterative methods, such as SIMP-based approaches, often suffer from high computational costs and slow convergence.
method_summary: Applies transformer architecture to one-shot topology optimization, using attention mechanisms to capture long-range structural dependencies and generate optimal topologies without iterative FEA.

title: A Foundation Model for Shape- and Resolution-Free Structural Topology Optimization
abstract: Structural topology optimization (TO) is central to engineering design but remains computationally intensive due to complex physics and hard constraints. Existing deep-learning methods are limited to fixed resolutions and domain shapes.
method_summary: Proposes the first foundation model for topology optimization that generalizes across arbitrary domain shapes and resolutions, trained on diverse structural problems.

title: Physics-Informed Transformer for Real-Time High-Fidelity Topology Optimization
abstract: Topology optimization is used for the design of high-performance structures but remains fundamentally limited by its iterative nature, requiring repeated finite element analyses that prevent real-time application.
method_summary: Combines physics-informed training with transformer architecture for real-time topology optimization, eliminating the need for iterative FEA during inference.

title: A Dual Physics-Informed Neural Network for Topology Optimization (DPNN-TO)
abstract: We propose a novel dual physics-informed neural network for topology optimization (DPNN-TO), which merges physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) with the traditional SIMP-based topology optimization. By embedding deep learning within the physical constraints, DPNN-TO eliminates the need for large-scale data and analytical sensitivity analysis.
method_summary: Integrates dual PINNs with SIMP-based topology optimization, using one network for displacement and another for density, eliminating need for training data and sensitivity analysis.

title: Gradient-Free Neural Topology Optimization
abstract: Gradient-free optimizers allow for tackling problems regardless of the smoothness or differentiability of their objective function, but they require many more iterations to converge when compared to gradient-based methods.
method_summary: Proposes a pre-trained neural reparameterization strategy that achieves order-of-magnitude iteration reduction for gradient-free topology optimization in latent space.

title: Latent Space Diffusion for Topology Optimization
abstract: Topology optimization enables the automated design of efficient structures by optimally distributing material within a defined domain. However, traditional gradient-based methods often scale poorly with problem size.
method_summary: Applies diffusion models in a learned latent space for scalable topology optimization, decoupling the generative process from mesh resolution to handle large-scale 3D problems.

title: Accelerating Metamaterial Topology Optimization Using Deep Super-Resolution Networks
abstract: To address this challenge, we propose a novel deep learning framework for metamaterial topology optimization using an enhanced deep super-resolution (EDSR) approach. Generating low-resolution topological designs and upscaling them.
method_summary: Uses deep super-resolution networks to upscale low-resolution topology optimization results for metamaterials, dramatically reducing computational cost while preserving fine structural details.

title: Diverse Topology Optimization Using Modulated Neural Fields (TOM)
abstract: To address this limitation, we introduce Topology Optimization using Modulated Neural Fields (TOM) -- a data-free method that trains a neural network to generate structurally compliant shapes and explore diverse design alternatives.
method_summary: Uses modulated neural fields to generate diverse topology-optimized designs from a single training run, enabling design space exploration without pre-computed datasets.

title: GANTL: Towards Practical and Real-Time Topology Optimization with Conditional GANs and Transfer Learning
abstract: The proposed method combines the generative power of conditional GANs with the knowledge transfer capabilities of transfer learning methods to predict optimal topologies for unseen boundary conditions.
method_summary: Combines conditional GANs with transfer learning for real-time topology optimization, enabling rapid prediction of optimal structures for new boundary conditions without retraining.

title: Accelerated Topology Optimization Design of 3D Structures Based on Deep Learning
abstract: To train the neural network, the Simplified Isotropic Material with Penalization (SIMP) method was adopted to generate the dataset of the optimized structures under randomness loading conditions and volume fractions.
method_summary: Uses 3D encoder-decoder CNN trained on SIMP-generated datasets to directly predict 3D topology-optimized structures, achieving orders of magnitude speedup over iterative methods.

title: TOuNN: Topology Optimization using Neural Networks
abstract: Neural networks, and more broadly, machine learning techniques, have been recently exploited to accelerate topology optimization through data-driven training and image processing. In this paper, we demonstrate an alternate use of neural networks.
method_summary: Uses neural networks as continuous density field representations within the optimization loop, replacing discrete element-wise design variables with a smooth neural parameterization.

title: Multiphysics Design Optimization via Generative Adversarial Networks
abstract: This work presents a method for generating concept designs for coupled multiphysics problems by employing generative adversarial networks (GANs).
method_summary: Applies GANs to multi-physics topology optimization problems involving coupled thermal-structural objectives, generating designs that satisfy multiple physics constraints simultaneously.

title: Multi-Material Multi-Physics Topology Optimization with Physics-Informed Gaussian Process Priors
abstract: Machine learning (ML) has been increasingly used for topology optimization (TO). However, most existing ML-based approaches focus on simplified benchmark problems due to their high computational cost.
method_summary: Introduces physics-informed Gaussian process priors for multi-material, multi-physics topology optimization, scaling ML-based TO to realistic engineering problems.

title: Toward Large Language Model-Driven Symbolic Topology Optimisation for Rapid Structural Concept Generation in Manufacturable Design
venue: Journal of Manufacturing and Materials Processing
abstract: This work explores the use of large language models for symbolic topology optimization, enabling rapid generation of structural concepts that satisfy manufacturability constraints.
method_summary: Pioneers LLM-driven symbolic topology optimization where natural language specifications are translated into manufacturable structural designs through LLM reasoning and code generation.

title: A Multidisciplinary Design and Optimization (MDO) Agent Driven by Large Language Models
abstract: To accelerate mechanical design and enhance design quality and innovation, we present a Multidisciplinary Design and Optimization (MDO) Agent driven by Large Language Models (LLMs).
method_summary: Proposes an LLM-driven agent for multidisciplinary design optimization that orchestrates simulation tools, interprets results, and iteratively refines designs through natural language interaction.

title: Using Hand-Drawn Inputs for Diffusion-Based Topology Optimization
abstract: Topology optimization (TO) is employed in engineering to optimize structural performance while maximizing material efficiency. However, traditional TO methods incur significant computational and time costs.
method_summary: Enables intuitive topology optimization by conditioning diffusion models on hand-drawn sketches, bridging designer intent with automated structural optimization.

title: Two-Stage Multiobjective Topology Optimization Method via SwinUnet with Enhanced Generalization
abstract: Topology optimization is a critical tool for modern structural design, yet existing methods often prioritize single objectives (e.g., compliance minimization) and suffer from prohibitive computational costs.
method_summary: Proposes a two-stage approach using SwinUnet transformer for multi-objective topology optimization, achieving Pareto-optimal designs with enhanced generalization across problem instances.

title: Diffusion Models for Topology Optimization in 3D Printing Applications
abstract: In recent times, Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) and autoencoders have gained popularity as an alternative to traditional iterative topology optimization methods.
method_summary: Applies diffusion models to 3D-printing-aware topology optimization, generating structures that are both structurally optimal and directly manufacturable via additive manufacturing.

title: A Perspective on AI-Enhanced Problem Solving in Engineering: Generative AI and Optimization
abstract: While optimization shines at precisely identifying highly-optimal solutions, GenAI excels at inferring problem requirements, bridging solution domains, handling mixed data modalities, and rapidly generating diverse candidate solutions.
method_summary: Provides a framework for combining generative AI with traditional optimization in engineering design, identifying complementary strengths and proposing hybrid workflows.

title: Benchmarking Generative AI Against Bayesian Optimization for Constrained Multi-Objective Inverse Design
abstract: This paper investigates the performance of Large Language Models (LLMs) as generative optimizers for solving constrained multi-objective regression tasks, specifically within the challenging domain of engineering inverse design.
method_summary: Benchmarks LLMs against Bayesian optimization for constrained multi-objective engineering design, revealing strengths and limitations of generative AI approaches for inverse design.

title: Towards Goal, Feasibility, and Diversity-Oriented Deep Generative Models in Design
abstract: Advancing DGMs in engineering design requires new training objectives which promote engineering design objectives. In this paper, we present the first Deep Generative Model that simultaneously optimizes for goal achievement, feasibility, and diversity.
method_summary: Proposes training objectives for deep generative models that simultaneously optimize design performance, physical feasibility, and solution diversity for engineering applications.

title: Towards Generative Design Using Optimal Transport for Shape Exploration and Solution Field Interpolation
abstract: We propose a generative design framework using optimal transport for shape exploration and solution field interpolation, enabling smooth navigation of the design space.
method_summary: Uses optimal transport theory for generative design, enabling continuous interpolation between structural shapes and their associated physical solution fields for design space exploration.

title: Generative AI-Based Prompt Evolution Engineering Design Optimization with Vision-Language Model
abstract: Engineering design optimization requires an efficient combination of a 3D shape representation, an optimization algorithm, and a design performance evaluation method, which is often computationally expensive.
method_summary: Proposes using vision-language models with evolutionary prompt optimization for engineering design, where natural language prompts guide 3D shape generation toward optimal performance.

title: Evolving Through the Looking Glass: Learning Improved Search Spaces with Variational Autoencoders
abstract: We show how generative machine learning can learn a representation corresponding to a valid region of search space, enabling optimizers to search in the new latent space and always find solutions that satisfy constraints.
method_summary: Uses VAEs to learn valid design spaces for engineering optimization, ensuring that evolutionary search in latent space always produces feasible designs satisfying manufacturing constraints.

title: Sequential Deep Operator Networks (S-DeepONet) for Predicting Full-Field Solutions Under Time-Dependent Loads
abstract: Results show that once trained, the proposed DeepONets can accurately predict the final full-field solution over the entire domain and are at least two orders of magnitude faster than direct finite element simulations.
method_summary: Extends DeepONet with sequential architecture for time-dependent loading, enabling full-field structural response prediction under arbitrary load histories at 100x speedup over FEA.

title: Graph Neural Networks as Fast and High-Fidelity Emulators for Finite-Element Ice Sheet Modeling
abstract: These GNNs reproduce ice thickness and velocity with better accuracy than the classic convolutional neural network (CNN) and multi-layer perception (MLP). In particular, GNNs successfully capture the complex dynamics.
method_summary: Demonstrates GNN-based emulators for finite-element ice sheet models, achieving higher fidelity than CNN/MLP alternatives while maintaining computational efficiency for climate simulations.

title: Discovering Explicit Reynolds-Averaged Turbulence Closures Through Deep Learning-Based Symbolic Regression
abstract: This work introduces a novel data-driven framework to formulate explicit algebraic Reynolds-averaged Navier-Stokes (RANS) turbulence closures. Recent years have witnessed a blossom in applying machine learning to turbulence modeling.
method_summary: Combines deep learning with symbolic regression to discover interpretable, explicit RANS turbulence closure models from high-fidelity simulation data.

title: Simultaneous and Meshfree Topology Optimization with Physics-Informed Gaussian Processes
abstract: Topology optimization (TO) provides a principled mathematical approach for optimizing the performance of a structure by designing its material spatial distribution in a pre-defined domain and subject to constraints.
method_summary: Proposes meshfree topology optimization using physics-informed Gaussian processes, simultaneously solving the state equation and optimizing the design without FEA discretization.

title: Deep Generative Design for Mass Production
abstract: Despite its success, generative design (GD) faces significant challenges regarding the manufacturability of complex designs, often necessitating extensive manual modifications due to limitations in standard manufacturing processes. This paper proposes a deep generative design framework that integrates manufacturing constraints directly into the generative process.
method_summary: Proposes a deep generative design framework that embeds manufacturing constraints (injection molding, casting, machining) into the design generation process, enabling mass-producible designs without post-hoc manual modification.

title: A Deep 3D Convolutional Neural Network Based Design for Manufacturability Framework
abstract: Traditionally, design for manufacturability (DFM) rules are hand-crafted and used to accelerate the engineering product design cycle by integrating manufacturability analysis during the design stage. This paper proposes a deep 3D CNN-based DFM framework that learns to predict manufacturability directly from 3D voxelized CAD models.
method_summary: Pioneering work using deep 3D CNNs to learn DFM rules directly from voxelized CAD models, replacing hand-crafted manufacturability rules with learned representations.

title: A Machine-Learning Framework for Design for Manufacturability
abstract: Computer-aided Design for Manufacturing (DFM) systems play an important role in reducing the time taken for product development by providing manufacturability feedback to the designer while a component is being designed. This paper presents a machine learning framework for DFM that can learn from manufacturing data.
method_summary: Introduces a general ML framework for DFM that learns manufacturability assessment from historical manufacturing data, enabling automated feedback during the design phase.

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks-Based Tolerance-Aware Manufacturability Assessment Integrating Design-for-Manufacturing Principles
abstract: This study proposes a methodology that evaluates manufacturability directly from parametric design features, enabling explicit incorporation of dimensional tolerances without requiring computer-aided design (CAD) geometry reconstruction. The approach employs Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (KANs) to learn functional relationships between design parameters, tolerances, and manufacturability outcomes.
method_summary: Uses Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (KANs) to assess manufacturability from parametric design features with explicit tolerance incorporation, bypassing the need for full CAD geometry reconstruction.

title: McGAN: Generating Manufacturable Designs by Embedding Manufacturing Rules into Conditional Generative Adversarial Network
abstract: Generative design methods aim to automatically generate a wide variety of designs that satisfy functional or aesthetic design requirements. However, research to date generally lacks consideration of manufacturability for the generated designs. This paper proposes a novel GD approach using deep neural networks to encode DFM rules into a conditional GAN.
method_summary: Embeds manufacturing rules (DFM constraints) directly into a conditional GAN architecture, enabling generation of designs that are inherently manufacturable without post-processing.

title: Enhancing the Product Quality of the Injection Process Using eXplainable Artificial Intelligence
abstract: This study proposes an optimal injection molding process control system to reduce the defect rate of injection molding products with XAI (eXplainable Artificial Intelligence) approaches. Boosting algorithms are used to predict defect occurrence and SHAP-based explanations identify critical process parameters.
method_summary: Combines boosting ML models with SHAP-based explainability for injection molding defect prediction, enabling interpretable process parameter optimization to reduce defect rates.

title: Explainable Artificial Intelligence for Manufacturing Cost Estimation and Machining Feature Visualization
abstract: The proposed process consists of (1) data collection and pre-processing, (2) 3D deep learning architecture exploration, and (3) visualization to explain the prediction results. The proposed deep learning model predicts manufacturing cost directly from 3D CAD models.
method_summary: Uses 3D deep learning on CAD models for manufacturing cost estimation with explainable AI visualization of machining features that drive cost predictions.

title: Machine Learning-Based Manufacturing Cost Prediction from 2D Engineering Drawings via Geometric Features
abstract: We present an integrated machine learning framework that transforms how manufacturing cost is estimated from 2D engineering drawings. Unlike traditional quotation workflows that require labor-intensive manual feature extraction, our approach automatically extracts geometric features from drawings.
method_summary: Automates manufacturing cost prediction from 2D engineering drawings by extracting geometric features and applying ML models, replacing labor-intensive manual quotation workflows.

title: Real-time prediction and adaptive adjustment of continuous casting based on deep learning
abstract: To address the challenge of real-time quality control in continuous casting, surrogate models are developed to replace high-fidelity simulations with acceptable accuracy and less computational time and cost. This work presents a deep learning-based surrogate for real-time casting defect prediction and adaptive process adjustment.
method_summary: Develops a deep learning surrogate model for real-time continuous casting quality prediction, enabling adaptive process adjustment with orders-of-magnitude speedup over physics simulations.

title: Data-Driven Prediction of Casting Defects in Magnesium High-Pressure Die Casting Using Machine Learning
abstract: The aim was to predict the presence of defect classes (cold flow, shrinkage cavity, blister, soldering point, scrap) in magnesium high-pressure die casting using machine learning models trained on process data.
method_summary: Applies ML classifiers to predict multiple casting defect types in Mg die casting from process parameters, enabling proactive quality control.

title: Springback prediction using machine learning: an application for simplified automotive body-in-white structures
abstract: Nonlinear FEA is used to predict springback in sheet metal forming, but it is time-consuming and requires specialized expertise. Machine learning holds the promise of providing rapid and accurate springback predictions for design exploration in automotive body-in-white structures.
method_summary: Trains ML models as surrogates for nonlinear FEA to rapidly predict springback in automotive sheet metal forming, enabling faster design exploration.

title: An Artificial Intelligence Application for In-Process Springback Control of Sheet Metal Bending
venue: ASME J. Manufacturing Science and Engineering
abstract: This paper presents an AI application for in-process springback control of sheet metal bending, using neural networks to predict and compensate for springback in real-time during the bending process.
method_summary: Applies neural networks for real-time in-process springback prediction and compensation during sheet metal bending, closing the loop between sensing and control.

title: Advancing Additive Manufacturing through Deep Learning: A Comprehensive Review of Current Progress and Future Challenges
abstract: This paper presents the first comprehensive literature review of deep learning (DL) applications in additive manufacturing (AM). It addresses the need for a thorough analysis in this rapidly growing area, covering design optimization, process monitoring, defect detection, and quality control.
method_summary: Comprehensive survey of deep learning in additive manufacturing covering design, process optimization, monitoring, and quality control, identifying trends toward generative models and physics-informed approaches.

title: Concurrent Build Direction, Part Segmentation, and Topology Optimization for Additive Manufacturing Using Neural Networks
abstract: We propose a neural network-based approach to topology optimization that aims to reduce the use of support structures in additive manufacturing. Our approach uses a network architecture that allows the concurrent optimization of build direction, part segmentation, and topology.
method_summary: Uses neural networks to concurrently optimize build direction, part segmentation, and topology for AM, significantly reducing support structure requirements.

title: Graph Neural Network-Based Topology Optimization for Self-Supporting Structures in Additive Manufacturing
abstract: This paper presents a machine learning-based framework for topology optimization of self-supporting structures, specifically tailored for additive manufacturing (AM). By employing a graph neural network (GNN), the framework learns to generate optimized topologies that satisfy overhang angle constraints.
method_summary: Proposes a GNN-based topology optimization framework that directly generates self-supporting structures for AM, eliminating the need for iterative overhang constraint enforcement.

title: Learn to Rotate: Part Orientation for Reducing Support Volume via Generalizable Reinforcement Learning
abstract: In design for additive manufacturing, an essential task is to determine the optimal build orientation of a part according to one or multiple factors. Heuristic search is used by most part orientation algorithms but is computationally expensive. This work proposes a generalizable reinforcement learning approach for part orientation optimization.
method_summary: Applies reinforcement learning to learn a generalizable policy for part build orientation optimization in AM, reducing support volume while being computationally efficient at inference time.

title: A Reinforcement Learning Approach for Process Parameter Optimization in Additive Manufacturing
abstract: This paper presents a reinforcement learning approach for optimizing process parameters in additive manufacturing. The RL agent learns to select optimal parameters (laser power, scan speed, layer thickness) to minimize defects while maintaining part quality.
method_summary: Formulates AM process parameter optimization as an RL problem, where an agent learns to dynamically adjust laser power, scan speed, and other parameters to minimize defects.

title: Multiscale Topology Optimization of Functionally Graded Lattice Structures Based on Physics-Augmented Neural Network Material Models
abstract: We present a new framework for the simultaneous optimization of both the topology as well as the relative density grading of cellular structures and materials, also known as lattices. Due to manufacturing constraints in additive manufacturing, the framework incorporates physics-augmented neural network material models.
method_summary: Combines physics-augmented neural networks with multiscale topology optimization to simultaneously optimize lattice topology and density grading under AM manufacturing constraints.

title: Generative Artificial Intelligence in Lattice Structure Design for Additive Manufacturing: A Critical Review
abstract: AI enables automated inverse design, generating lattice structures that meet predefined functional and mechanical targets, reducing trial-and-error efforts. It supports performance optimization by enhancing mechanical, thermal, and acoustic properties through data-driven approaches.
method_summary: Reviews generative AI methods (GANs, VAEs, diffusion models) for lattice structure design in AM, covering inverse design, performance optimization, and process-aware generation.

title: LatticeGraphNet: A Two-Scale Graph Neural Operator for Simulating Lattice Structures
abstract: This study introduces a two-scale Graph Neural Operator (GNO), namely LatticeGraphNet (LGN), designed as a surrogate model for costly nonlinear finite-element simulations of three-dimensional lattice structures.
method_summary: Proposes a two-scale graph neural operator as a fast surrogate for nonlinear FE simulation of 3D lattice structures, enabling rapid mechanical response prediction for design optimization.

title: Predictive Modeling of Lattice Structure Design for 316L Stainless Steel Using Machine Learning in the L-PBF Process
abstract: Lattice structures in additive manufacturing of 316L stainless steel have gained increasing attention due to their well-suited mechanical properties and lightweight characteristics. Infill structures play a critical role in determining the mechanical performance of printed parts.
method_summary: Develops ML models to predict mechanical properties of lattice structures in L-PBF from design parameters, enabling rapid design space exploration for 316L stainless steel parts.

title: Deep Learning for Melt Pool Depth Contour Prediction From Surface Thermal Images via Vision Transformers
abstract: Insufficient overlap between the melt pools produced during Laser Powder Bed Fusion (L-PBF) can lead to lack-of-fusion defects and deteriorated mechanical and fatigue performance. In-situ monitoring of melt pool depth is critical but challenging. This work uses Vision Transformers to predict melt pool depth contours from surface thermal images.
method_summary: Applies Vision Transformers to predict subsurface melt pool depth contours from surface thermal images in L-PBF, enabling real-time in-situ monitoring for defect prevention.

title: Additive Manufacturing Processes Protocol Prediction by Artificial Intelligence using X-ray Computed Tomography Data
abstract: The quality of the part fabricated from the Additive Manufacturing (AM) process depends upon the process parameters used, and therefore, optimization is required for apt quality. A methodology is proposed to predict AM process protocols from X-ray CT scan data using AI.
method_summary: Proposes an AI methodology to predict optimal AM process protocols from X-ray CT data, linking internal part quality metrics to process parameter selection.

title: Accelerated Discovery of 3D Printing Materials Using Data-Driven Multiobjective Optimization
abstract: We propose a machine learning approach to accelerating the discovery of additive manufacturing materials with optimal trade-offs in mechanical performance. A multiobjective optimization algorithm automatically navigates the vast design space of material compositions and process parameters.
method_summary: Combines Bayesian multiobjective optimization with ML surrogates to accelerate discovery of 3D printing materials, achieving Pareto-optimal mechanical properties with minimal experiments.

title: Self-supporting Topology Optimization for Additive Manufacturing
abstract: The paper presents a topology optimization approach that designs an optimal structure, called a self-supporting structure, which is ready to be fabricated via additive manufacturing without the usage of additional support structures.
method_summary: Introduces an overhang angle constraint formulation for topology optimization that directly produces self-supporting structures for AM, eliminating support structure requirements.

title: Holistic Review on Design for Additive Manufacturing
abstract: The review explores DfAM potential, tools, standards, and applications and key insights/inferences are presented. The review presented 14 major DfAM insights and suggested areas for further development across design methods, tools, and standards.
method_summary: Comprehensive review of DfAM covering design methods, computational tools, standards, and industrial applications, identifying 14 key insights and future research directions.

title: Fabrica: Dual-Arm Assembly of General Multi-Part Objects via Integrated Planning and Learning
abstract: Multi-part assembly poses significant challenges for robotic systems to execute long-horizon, contact-rich manipulation with generalization across complex geometries. We present a dual-arm robotic system that unifies long-horizon planning and robust contact-rich control for assembling general multi-part objects directly from CAD models.
method_summary: Integrates task-and-motion planning with learned contact-rich manipulation policies for dual-arm robotic assembly directly from CAD models, achieving generalization across diverse object geometries. CoRL 2025 Best Paper.

title: Physics-Aware Combinatorial Assembly Sequence Planning using Data-free Action Masking
abstract: This paper studies assembly sequence planning (ASP) for physical combinatorial assembly. Given the shape of the desired object, the goal is to find a sequence of actions for placing unit primitives to construct the target shape while respecting physical stability constraints.
method_summary: Proposes a deep RL approach with physics-aware data-free action masking for combinatorial assembly sequence planning, ensuring physical feasibility without requiring simulation during training.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning Applied to an Assembly Sequence Planning Problem with User Preferences
abstract: Deep reinforcement learning (DRL) has demonstrated its potential in solving complex manufacturing decision-making problems, especially in a context where the system learns over time with active human feedback. This paper applies DRL to assembly sequence planning incorporating user preferences.
method_summary: Applies deep RL to assembly sequence planning with human preference integration, enabling the system to learn user-preferred assembly strategies while satisfying feasibility constraints.

title: ASAP: Automated Sequence Planning for Complex Robotic Assembly with Physical Feasibility
abstract: ASAP: Automated Sequence Planning for Complex Robotic Assembly with Physical Feasibility. This work presents an automated approach to generating physically feasible assembly sequences for complex multi-part objects.
method_summary: Proposes ASAP, an automated assembly sequence planner that generates physically feasible plans for complex robotic assembly by combining geometric reasoning with learned feasibility prediction.

title: Optimal Robotic Assembly Sequence Planning (ORASP)
abstract: The optimal robotic assembly planning problem entails determining the sequence of actions for a robot to feasibly assemble a product from its components which minimizes a given objective. The problem is challenging due to the exponentially vast number of possible plans.
method_summary: Formulates optimal robotic assembly sequence planning as a combinatorial optimization problem and proposes efficient search algorithms with learned heuristics to find optimal plans.

title: Hierarchical Hybrid Learning for Long-Horizon Contact-Rich Robotic Assembly
abstract: Generalizable long-horizon robotic assembly requires reasoning at multiple levels of abstraction. While end-to-end imitation learning (IL) is a promising approach, it typically requires large amounts of demonstration data. This work proposes hierarchical hybrid learning combining planning with learned skills.
method_summary: Combines hierarchical task planning with learned contact-rich manipulation skills for long-horizon robotic assembly, achieving data efficiency through hybrid planning-learning architecture.

title: 3D Combinatorial Construction with Deep Reinforcement Learning
abstract: To address the challenges of 3D combinatorial construction, we propose a two-part deep reinforcement learning (DRL) framework that tackles teaching the robot to understand the objective of an incomplete assembly and learning a construction policy.
method_summary: Proposes a two-part DRL framework for 3D combinatorial construction where one network understands assembly goals and another learns construction policies for sequential block placement.

title: REASSEMBLE: A Multimodal Dataset for Contact-rich Robotic Assembly and Disassembly
abstract: Built around the NIST Assembly Task Board 1 benchmark, REASSEMBLE includes four actions (pick, insert, remove, and place) involving 17 objects. The dataset contains 4,551 demonstrations, of which 4,035 are successful, with multimodal sensing data.
method_summary: Introduces a large-scale multimodal dataset for contact-rich robotic assembly/disassembly with 4,551 demonstrations across 17 objects, providing force, vision, and audio data for learning-based assembly.

title: Tolerance Allocation of Complex Systems Based on Supervised Machine Learning and Adaptive Sampling
venue: International Journal of Advanced Manufacturing Technology
abstract: This paper presents a novel framework for the tolerance allocation of complex systems. The framework integrates adaptive sampling, importance sampling, and the C4.5 decision tree algorithm to enhance tolerance allocation efficiency and accuracy.
method_summary: Combines supervised ML (C4.5 decision trees) with adaptive sampling for efficient tolerance allocation in complex assemblies, reducing computational cost compared to Monte Carlo methods.

title: Genetic Algorithm Optimization Based on Manufacturing Prediction for an Efficient Tolerance Allocation Approach
abstract: The tolerance allocation is an extremely sensitive task due to the complex effects on quality, product, and cost. This paper proposes a GA optimization approach based on manufacturing prediction models for efficient tolerance allocation covering design and manufacturing aspects.
method_summary: Integrates ML-based manufacturing prediction models with genetic algorithm optimization for tolerance allocation, jointly optimizing quality and cost.

title: Statistical Tolerance Allocation Design Considering Form Errors Based on Rigid Assembly Simulation and Deep Q-Network
venue: International Journal of Advanced Manufacturing Technology
abstract: In this paper, a statistical tolerance allocation method is presented that considers form errors. A combination of rigid assembly simulation and deep Q-network is used to optimize tolerance allocation for complex products.
method_summary: Applies Deep Q-Network reinforcement learning to statistical tolerance allocation considering form errors, using rigid assembly simulation for reward computation.

title: Learning to Generate Fine-Grained Robotic Assembly Instructions from Multi-View Images
abstract: We propose an end-to-end model known as the Neural Assembler. This model learns an object graph where each vertex represents recognized components from the images, and the edges specify assembly relationships and instructions.
method_summary: Proposes Neural Assembler, an end-to-end model that generates fine-grained assembly instructions from multi-view images by learning object graphs with component relationships.

title: Automatic Feature Recognition and Dimensional Attributes Extraction From CAD Models for Hybrid Additive-Subtractive Manufacturing
abstract: The Hierarchical Graph Convolutional Neural Network (HGCNN) model is implemented to accurately identify the composite additive-subtractive features within the synthetic CAD dataset. This enables automated process planning for hybrid manufacturing.
method_summary: Uses hierarchical graph CNNs to recognize manufacturing features in CAD models for hybrid additive-subtractive manufacturing, automating the feature recognition step of process planning.

title: BrepMFR: Enhancing Machining Feature Recognition in B-rep Models through Deep Learning and Domain Adaptation
abstract: We propose a novel deep learning network, BrepMFR, designed for Machining Feature Recognition (MFR) from Boundary Representation (B-rep) models. We transform the original B-rep model into a graph representation leveraging Transformer architecture and graph attention mechanism.
method_summary: Proposes BrepMFR using graph Transformers with attention mechanisms for machining feature recognition from B-Rep CAD models, with domain adaptation to bridge synthetic-to-real gaps.

title: Self-Supervised Masked BRep Autoencoders for Machining Feature Recognition
abstract: Machining feature recognition (MFR) is a technique that automatically extracts geometric shapes with specific machining semantics, such as holes, slots, bosses, and chamfers, from 3D CAD models. This work proposes self-supervised masked BRep autoencoders for pre-training representations useful for MFR.
method_summary: Introduces self-supervised pre-training via masked BRep autoencoders for machining feature recognition, learning transferable CAD representations without labeled data.

title: Transformer-Based B-rep Geometric Feature Recognition
abstract: We propose a Transformer-based approach for B-rep geometric feature recognition. In addition, we also proposed a dataset named Complex B-rep Feature Dataset (CBF), comprising 20,000 B-rep models covering more complex B-rep models aligned with industrial applications.
method_summary: Applies Transformer architecture to B-Rep geometric feature recognition with a new 20K-model dataset (CBF) covering industrially complex geometries beyond existing benchmarks.

title: BRepGAT: Graph Neural Network to Segment Machining Feature Faces in a B-rep Model
abstract: In recent years, there have been many studies using artificial intelligence to recognize machining features in three-dimensional models in the CAD/CAM domain. This work proposes BRepGAT, a graph attention network for segmenting machining feature faces in B-rep models.
method_summary: Proposes BRepGAT using graph attention networks for face-level segmentation of machining features in B-Rep CAD models, enabling fine-grained feature boundary identification.

title: Real-Time Tool-Path Planning Using Deep Learning for Subtractive Manufacturing
abstract: Tool-path planning is a crucial factor of computer-aided design (CAD) and computer-aided manufacturing (CAM). Previous path generation methods often transform the problem into local or global optimization. This work proposes a deep learning approach for real-time toolpath planning.
method_summary: Applies deep learning for real-time CNC toolpath planning, replacing iterative optimization with a learned model that generates near-optimal paths in milliseconds.

title: Data-driven Modelling of Machine Tool Feedrate Behavior with Neural Networks
abstract: Usually, Computer Aided Manufacturing (CAM) software estimates the machining times using the commanded feedrate from the toolpath file using basic kinematic settings. This paper proposes neural network models to predict actual machine tool feedrate behavior for more accurate machining time estimation.
method_summary: Trains neural networks to predict actual CNC machine feedrate behavior from toolpath data, improving machining time estimation accuracy over conventional CAM kinematic models.

title: Large Language Models for Manufacturing
abstract: This paper provides a comprehensive exploration of the integration of LLMs into the manufacturing domain, focusing on their potential to automate and enhance various aspects of manufacturing, from process planning to quality control and supply chain management.
method_summary: Surveys LLM applications in manufacturing including process planning automation, G-code generation, quality inspection, and supply chain optimization, identifying opportunities and hallucination risks.

title: Knowledge Graph Fusion with Large Language Models for Accurate, Explainable Manufacturing Process Planning
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) promise flexible, instruction-driven reasoning for tasks such as G-code synthesis and spindle-load queries, but they routinely hallucinate numeric values and provide no provenance. This paper fuses knowledge graphs with LLMs for manufacturing process planning.
method_summary: Fuses manufacturing knowledge graphs with LLMs to ground process planning in verified facts, reducing hallucination in G-code generation and machining parameter selection.

title: Reinforcement Learning-Based Cutting Parameter Dynamic Decision Method Considering Tool Wear for a Turning Machining Process
venue: International Journal of Precision Engineering and Manufacturing-Green Technology
abstract: Cutting parameter optimization is considered as an effective way for energy consumption saving. In the machining process, the tool wear of cutting tools varies with the rise of the number of workpieces processed. This paper proposes an RL-based dynamic cutting parameter decision method.
method_summary: Applies reinforcement learning to dynamically adjust cutting parameters during turning based on real-time tool wear state, optimizing energy consumption and surface quality simultaneously.

title: Making Informed Decisions in Cutting Tool Maintenance in Milling
abstract: Tool Condition Monitoring (TCM) is vital for maintaining productivity and product quality in machining. This study leverages machine learning to analyze real-time force signals collected from experimental milling operations for tool wear prediction and maintenance decision-making.
method_summary: Uses ML on real-time force signals for tool condition monitoring in milling, enabling data-driven maintenance decisions that balance tool life and part quality.

title: Implicit Neural Fields for Collision-Free Multi-Axis 3D Printing
abstract: This unified approach enables toolpath optimization across both surface and interior domains, allowing shell and infill paths to be generated via implicit field interpolation. The method uses implicit neural fields to represent collision-free multi-axis printing toolpaths.
method_summary: Represents multi-axis 3D printing toolpaths as implicit neural fields, enabling collision-free path optimization that unifies shell and infill generation for complex geometries.

title: Hybrid Deep Learning Cost Evaluation Using CNN with ANN for the Plastic Injection Industry
abstract: These results can predict cost evaluation at about 98.65% accuracy for parts costs, 95.17% accuracy for mould costs, and 96.83% of multi-price for multi-volume. The hybrid CNN-ANN model processes both geometric and process features.
method_summary: Combines CNN (for geometric feature extraction) with ANN (for cost regression) in a hybrid architecture for plastic injection molding cost estimation, achieving over 95% accuracy.

title: Sample-Efficient Bayesian Transfer Learning for Online Machine Parameter Optimization
abstract: This work introduces a method to optimize the machine parameters in the system itself using a Bayesian optimization (BO) algorithm. By leveraging existing machine data, we use a transfer learning approach to achieve sample-efficient online parameter optimization.
method_summary: Combines Bayesian optimization with transfer learning for sample-efficient online optimization of manufacturing machine parameters, leveraging prior machine data to reduce required experiments.

title: An Additive Manufacturing Optimal Control Framework Demonstrated in Minimizing Layer-Level Thermal Variance in Electron Beam Powder Bed Fusion
abstract: Additive manufacturing (AM) techniques hold promise but face significant challenges in process planning and optimization. The large temporal and spatial variations in temperature that can occur in layer-by-layer fabrication motivate optimal control approaches.
method_summary: Proposes an optimal control framework for AM that minimizes layer-level thermal variance in electron beam PBF using learned thermal models, improving part quality consistency.

title: PhysiX: A Foundation Model for Physics Simulations
abstract: We introduce PhysiX, a family of large-scale foundation models for physics simulation. PhysiX is an autoregressive generative model composed of a discrete tokenizer, which converts heterogeneous physical processes at different scales into a sequence of discrete tokens, and employs an autoregressive next-token prediction objective to model such processes.
method_summary: 4.5B-parameter autoregressive foundation model that tokenizes heterogeneous physics simulations (fluids, solids, multi-physics) into discrete tokens and uses next-token prediction, achieving cross-domain generalization without task-specific fine-tuning.

title: OmniArch: Building Foundation Model for Scientific Computing
abstract: Foundation models have revolutionized language modeling, while whether this success is replicated in scientific computing remains unexplored. We present OmniArch, the first prototype aiming at solving diverse PDEs across different physics domains with a single unified model.
method_summary: First unified foundation model for multi-physics PDE solving, using an axis-based encoder and decoder-only Transformer to handle diverse scientific computing tasks (heat, fluid, elasticity) in a single architecture.

title: Unified Physics Transformers: A Framework For Efficiently Scaling Neural Operators
abstract: With ever increasing problem complexity, the natural question arises: what is an efficient way to scale neural operators to larger and more complex simulations. We propose Unified Physics Transformers (UPT) that encode data flexibly into a compressed latent space representation, allowing efficient operation without relying on grid or particle-based latent spaces.
method_summary: Proposes Unified Physics Transformers that compress heterogeneous physics data (grids, particles, meshes) into a unified latent space, enabling efficient scaling of neural operators to larger simulations.

title: Towards a Physics Foundation Model
abstract: Access to a Physics Foundation Model (PFM) would be transformative -- democratizing access to high-fidelity simulations, accelerating scientific discovery, and eliminating the need for specialized solvers. We propose a roadmap toward building such a model, identifying key challenges in data, architecture, and evaluation.
method_summary: Proposes a roadmap for Physics Foundation Models, analyzing architectural choices (tokenization, attention, conditioning), data requirements, and evaluation protocols for cross-domain physics simulation.

title: PINNsAgent: Automated PDE Surrogation with Large Language Models
abstract: Solving partial differential equations (PDEs) using neural methods has been a long-standing pursuit. Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs) have emerged as a promising approach but require extensive manual tuning. We introduce PINNsAgent, a novel surrogation framework that leverages LLMs to automate PINN parameter tuning and architecture selection.
method_summary: Uses LLM agents to automate the configuration and tuning of PINNs for PDE solving, bridging domain-specific knowledge with deep learning through natural language interaction and automated hyperparameter search.

title: Physics-Informed Diffusion Models
abstract: We present a framework that unifies generative modeling and partial differential equation fulfillment by introducing a first-principle-based loss term that enforces generated samples to fulfill the underlying PDE constraints during the diffusion process.
method_summary: Unifies diffusion models with physics-informed constraints by embedding PDE residual losses into the denoising process, significantly improving physical consistency of generated samples for engineering simulation.

title: Multi-scale Topology Optimization using Neural Networks
abstract: We propose a new method for multi-scale topology optimization that seamlessly ensures compatibility across neighboring microstructure cells without the need for a catalog of unit cells and associated connectivity constraints. Our approach consists of a topology neural network that optimizes the microstructure shape and distribution across the design domain as a continuous field.
method_summary: Neural network-based multi-scale topology optimization that generates spatially varying microstructures as a continuous field, ensuring inter-cell compatibility without pre-computed unit cell catalogs. Published at SIGGRAPH.

title: Consistent Machine Learning for Topology Optimization with Microstructure-Dependent Neural Network Material Models
abstract: Additive manufacturing methods together with topology optimization have enabled the creation of multiscale structures with controlled spatially-varying material microstructure. This work presents a framework for designing multiscale heterogeneous structures by merging homogenization-based topology optimization with consistent neural network material models that enforce polyconvexity, objectivity, and thermodynamic consistency.
method_summary: Merges homogenization-based topology optimization with physics-consistent neural network material models (enforcing polyconvexity, objectivity, symmetry) for multiscale structure design with spatially varying microstructures.

title: Guiding Topology Optimization Diffusion with Human Preferences
abstract: Inspired by preference alignment techniques in generative modeling, our method learns to suppress unrealistic outputs by modulating the reverse diffusion trajectory using gradients of human-aligned reward models for topology optimization.
method_summary: Applies RLHF-style preference alignment to diffusion-based topology optimization, using human preference reward models to guide the generative process toward designs that match engineer expectations.

title: Agentic LLM Orchestration of Engineering Analysis in Product Development Design Practice
abstract: This paper presents an agentic LLM framework that orchestrates engineering analysis workflows in product development, including FEA setup, mesh generation, solver configuration, and result interpretation, through natural language interaction with CAE tools.
method_summary: Proposes an agentic LLM system that orchestrates end-to-end engineering analysis (FEA/CFD) in product development, automating CAE workflow from problem specification to result interpretation via natural language.

title: SimuAgent: An LLM-Based Simulink Modeling Assistant Enhanced with Reinforcement Learning
abstract: We introduce SimuAgent, an LLM-powered modeling and simulation agent tailored for Simulink. SimuAgent replaces verbose XML with a concise, dictionary-style Python representation, dramatically cutting context length. Trained with reflection-guided reinforcement learning, it plans and executes modeling tasks with high accuracy.
method_summary: LLM agent for Simulink modeling that uses compact Python representations and reflection-guided RL training to automate block diagram construction, parameter tuning, and simulation execution.

title: GRACE: Efficient and Feasible Robotic Assembly Sequence Planning via Graph Representation Learning
abstract: Automatic Robotic Assembly Sequence Planning (RASP) can significantly improve productivity and resilience in modern manufacturing. With GRACE, we extract meaningful information from graph input and predict assembly sequences in a step-by-step manner using graph attention networks.
method_summary: Uses graph attention networks for robotic assembly sequence planning, learning to predict feasible assembly orders from part relationship graphs with physical constraint satisfaction.

title: Deep Neural Implicit Representation of Accessibility for Multi-Axis Manufacturing
abstract: One of the main concerns in design and process planning for multi-axis additive and subtractive manufacturing is collision avoidance between moving objects and stationary objects. We develop an implicit representation of the collision measure field via deep neural networks that can accurately interpolate the collision measure for arbitrary tool orientations.
method_summary: Learns implicit neural representations of manufacturing accessibility/collision fields for multi-axis machining, enabling rapid collision checking for arbitrary tool orientations without explicit geometric computation.

title: DeepMill: Neural Accessibility Learning for Subtractive Manufacturing
abstract: We introduce DeepMill, the first neural framework designed to accurately and efficiently predict inaccessible and occlusion regions under varying machining tool parameters, applicable to both 3-axis and 5-axis CNC machining configurations.
method_summary: First neural framework for predicting machining accessibility and occlusion regions for 3-axis and 5-axis CNC, replacing expensive geometric computation with learned predictions that generalize across tool parameters.

title: Implicit Neural Field-Based Process Planning for Multi-Axis Manufacturing
abstract: We present an implicit neural field-based framework for multi-axis process planning that overcomes limitations of discrete layer-based approaches by embedding both layer generation and toolpath design within a single differentiable implicit field representation using sinusoidally activated neural networks.
method_summary: Embeds multi-axis manufacturing process planning (layer generation + toolpath design) into a single differentiable implicit neural field, enabling continuous optimization of the entire manufacturing plan.

title: Large Language Model-Empowered Next-Generation Computer-Aided Engineering
abstract: Software development has entered a new era where large language models now serve as general-purpose reasoning engines, enabling natural language interaction and transformative applications across engineering domains. This paper surveys how LLMs are reshaping computer-aided engineering including FEA automation, CFD workflow orchestration, and design optimization.
method_summary: Surveys the emerging paradigm of LLM-empowered CAE, covering automated FEA/CFD setup, natural language-driven simulation, intelligent post-processing, and the path toward autonomous engineering analysis agents.

title: Automating Data-Driven Modeling and Analysis for Engineering Applications using Large Language Model Agents
abstract: In this study, we propose an innovative pipeline utilizing Large Language Model agents to automate data-driven modeling and analysis, with emphasis on regression tasks for engineering applications including surrogate modeling and material property prediction.
method_summary: Proposes an LLM agent pipeline that automates the entire data-driven modeling workflow for engineering (data preprocessing, feature selection, model training, validation) through natural language instructions.

title: Fast and Accurate Bayesian Optimization with Pre-trained Transformers for Constrained Engineering Problems
abstract: Bayesian Optimization (BO) is a foundational strategy for efficiently handling black-box functions with many constraints and expensive evaluations. We propose using pre-trained transformer models as surrogate functions within BO, achieving faster convergence on constrained engineering design problems.
method_summary: Replaces traditional Gaussian process surrogates in Bayesian optimization with pre-trained transformers, achieving faster convergence on constrained engineering design problems with expensive evaluations.

title: Large Language Models for Combinatorial Optimization of Design Structure Matrix
abstract: In complex engineering systems, the dependencies between components create intricate coupling patterns that significantly impact development efficiency. We apply LLMs to optimize Design Structure Matrices, a fundamental tool in systems engineering for managing component interdependencies.
method_summary: Applies LLMs to combinatorial optimization of Design Structure Matrices in systems engineering, using natural language reasoning to reorder and cluster coupled engineering components for efficient development.

title: Connector-Aware General Robotic Assembly from Instruction Manuals via Vision-Language Models
abstract: Assembly hinges on reliably forming connections between parts; yet most robotic approaches plan assembly sequences and part poses while treating connectors as an afterthought. We propose a connector-aware assembly framework that uses vision-language models to parse instruction manuals and reason about connector types and mating constraints.
method_summary: Uses vision-language models to parse assembly instruction manuals and reason about connector types, enabling connector-aware robotic assembly planning that explicitly models mating constraints.

title: AssemMate: Graph-Based LLM for Robotic Assembly Assistance
abstract: This graph-based LLM enables knowledge graph question answering (KGQA), supporting human-robot interaction and assembly task planning for specific products. Beyond interactive QA, AssemMate also supports automated assembly sequence generation from product knowledge graphs.
method_summary: Combines knowledge graphs with LLMs for robotic assembly assistance, enabling natural language interaction for assembly planning and automated sequence generation from product structure knowledge.
